diagrams.title: Basic Venn Diagram - Union and Intersection
diagrams.description: A Venn diagram showing two overlapping circles representing sets A and B within a universal set rectangle. The diagram clearly labels the intersection (A ∩ B), union (A ∪ B), and the distinct regions for elements only in A, only in B, and in neither. This helps students visualize how union combines all elements while intersection shows shared elements.
elements: Rectangle representing Universal Set U
Circle A on the left
Circle B on the right with overlap
Intersection region A ∩ B shaded differently
Union region A ∪ B indicated
Labels for each region: A only, B only, A ∩ B, and outside both sets
Curly braces notation for each region
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concepts of union (∪) and intersection (∩) operations with visual representation of how elements are distributed between sets

diagrams.title: Set Difference and Complement Operations
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the difference operation (A - B or A \ B) and complement operation (A'). On the left, a Venn diagram highlights the region belonging to A but not B. On the right, the complement A' is shown as everything outside circle A but within the universal set. This clarifies that complement is relative to the universal set.
elements: Universal set rectangle U
Circle A and circle B overlapping
Shaded region for A - B (A minus B)
Shaded region for A' (complement of A)
Labels showing A - B = A \ B
Labels showing A' = U - A
Color coding to distinguish the operations
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates set difference (relative complement) and absolute complement operations, helping students understand how to find elements in one set but not another

diagrams.title: Three-Set Venn Diagram - Complex Operations
diagrams.description: A Venn diagram with three overlapping circles representing sets A, B, and C. This creates 7 distinct regions: only A, only B, only C, A∩B only, A∩C only, B∩C only, and A∩B∩C. The diagram shows how to compute operations like (A ∩ B) - C, (A ∪ B) ∩ C, and demonstrates the distributive laws visually.
elements: Universal set rectangle
Three circles A, B, C in triangular arrangement
All 7 regions labeled with set notation
Central region A ∩ B ∩ C highlighted
Labels for pairwise intersections
Labels for single-set regions
Example shaded region showing (A ∩ B) - C
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates complex set operations with three sets and demonstrates the distributive laws, De Morgan's laws, and how operations work with multiple sets
